title: Deficits and debts
description: Practice what you have learned about deficits and debts in this exercise.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following best describes the circumstances in which a government will run a budget deficit?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Tax revenues are less than the sum of the government spending component of real GDP and transfer payments
Tax revenues are more than the sum of the government spending component of real GDP and transfer payments
The accumulation of budget shortfalls over time
When a government engages in contractionary fiscal policy
When a government runs a high debt to income ratio

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following best describes the circumstance in which a government will run a budget surplus?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Tax revenues are more than the sum of the government spending (G) component of real GDP and transfer payments
Tax revenues are less than the sum of the Government spending (G) component of real GDP and transfer payments
The accumulation of budget shortfalls over time
When a government engages in expansionary fiscal policy
When a government runs a high debt to income ratio

assessment_items.question_content:
The government of Hamsterville had a balanced budget, but now is experiencing a recession. 

**Which of the following is most likely to occur as a result of the recession?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: A budget deficit
A budget surplus
A balanced budget
Less debt
Higher tax revenue

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following changes would cause a balanced budget to move to a budget surplus?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: A decrease in transfer payments
A decrease in national income
A decrease in tax revenue
An increase in transfer payments
An increase in government spending

assessment_items.question_content:
The government of Cloud City spent $\$100$ million more than it had in tax revenues this year. 

**What is the impact of this shortage on the budget balance and national debt in Cloud City?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: There is a budget deficit; the debt will increase
There is a budget deficit; no impact on the national debt
There is a budget surplus; no impact on the national debt
There is a balanced budget; national debt will increase
There is a budget surplus; national debt will decrease

assessment_items.question_content:
**If an economy initially has a balanced budget, but its transfer payments increase, what happens to the budget balance and national debt?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: There is a budget deficit; the debt will increase
There is a budget deficit; no impact on national debt
There is a budget surplus; no impact on national debt
There is a balanced budget; national debt will increase
There is a budget surplus; national debt will decrease

assessment_items.question_content:
The government of Tatooine had a balanced budget last year. However, an unanticipated recession triggered by the wanton destruction of Jabba’s Palace leads to a decrease in tax revenues, even though government spending and transfer payments did not change. 

**What happens to the budget balance and national debt in Tatooine as a result of the decrease in tax revenues?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: There is a budget deficit; the debt will increase
There is a budget deficit; no impact on national debt
There is a budget surplus; no impact on national debt
There is a balanced budget; national debt will increase
There is a budget surplus; national debt will decrease

assessment_items.question_content:
Chewbacca noticed that last year, the budget on his homeland Kashyyyk was balanced, while this year, its national debt increased.

**Which of the following scenarios would most likely have caused the increase in national debt?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Government spending increased by $\$100$ million and tax revenue increased by $\$60$ million
Government spending increased by $\$100$ million and tax revenue increased by $\$100$ million
Transfer payments increased by $\$20$ million and tax revenue increased by an equal amount $\$20$ million
Transfer payments increased by $\$20$ million and tax revenue increased by $\$60$ million
Government spending increased by $\$20$ million and transfer payments decreased by $\$20$ million with no change in tax revenue

assessment_items.question_content:
The national debt on Naboo is $\$100$ million, and the interest rate on that debt is $4\%$. When preparing the national budget, the government of Naboo forgot to include any provisions for paying down the national debt or any interest payments.

**If the government of Naboo wants to maintain a balanced budget and not add to the national debt, which of the following actions will be necessary?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
The interest payments on national debt are:

$\begin{align}\text{Interest payment on debt} &= \text{Amount of debt} \times \text{interest rate}\\\\
&=\$100 \text{ million} \times 4\%\\\\
&=\$4 \text{ million}\end{align}$

choices.content: Decrease government spending by at least $\$4$ million
Increase government spending by at least $\$4$ million
Increase transfer payments by at least $\$4$ million
Increase tax revenues by $\$1$ million
Decrease transfer payments by $\$1$ million and raise tax revenues by $\$2$ million

assessment_items.question_content:
In 2016 the national debt on Naboo was $\$100$ million. Naboo pays $4\%$ annual interest on that debt, but debt payments were not included in the national budget. The table below shows the tax revenues, government spending, and transfer payments from 2017. 

Tax revenue | government spending | transfer payments
-|-|-
$\$300$ million | $\$250$ million | $\$50$ million

**If Naboo’s government does not want to add to its debt and have a balanced budget in 2017, which of the following actions will achieve those objectives?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Reduce government spending to $\$246$ million or less
Increase government spending to at least $\$254$ million
Sell newly issued government bonds
Reduce transfer payments by $\$3$ million
Increase tax revenue by $\$3$ million

assessment_items.question_content:
The government of Coruscant currently has a balanced budget. There is a national debt of $\$500$ million which incurs $10\%$ interest each year, but interest payments on the national debt are not currently included in the national budget.

**Which of the following will be necessary to reduce the size of the national debt?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Generate a surplus of more than $\$50$ million
Maintain a balanced budget
Reduce government spending by $\$50$ million
Reduce transfer payments by $\$50$ million
Increase tax revenues by $\$50$ million

assessment_items.question_content:
The government of Kashyyyk has a $\$200$ million national debt which accrues $10\%$ interest annually. The government would like to maintain a balanced budget and reduce the size of the national debt, but also does not want to change transfer payments.

**Which of the following actions will achieve the government of Kashyyyk’s goals?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Increase tax revenues by more than $\$20$ million
Issue government bonds
Increase tax revenues by $\$20$ million
Borrow money from foreign entities to make interest payments
Borrow money from another government agency
